channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: US charges Chinese hackers, government officials in broad cybercrime campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Twelve Chinese nationals, including mercenary hackers, law enforcement officers and employees of a private hacking company, have been charged in connection with global cybercrime campaigns targeting dissidents, news organizations, U.S.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Scientists raise concerns as the US stops sharing air quality data from embassies worldwide
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — The U.S. government will stop sharing air quality data gathered from its embassies and consulates, worrying local scientists and experts who say the effort was vital to monitor global air quality and improve public health.
item.dc:creator: TAIWO ADEBAYO and SIBI ARASU, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Protests turn violent as Greece's government battles censure motion over deadly train crash
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — Protesters launched gasoline bombs and fireworks in clashes with police outside Greece's parliament late Wednesday, in renewed nationwide protests calling for politicians to be held accountable for a 2023 rail disaster that claimed 57 lives.
item.dc:creator: DEREK GATOPOULOS and THANASSIS STAVRAKIS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump administration deletes list of hundreds of federal buildings targeted for potential sale
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Trump administration on Tuesday published a list of more than 440 federal properties it had identified to potentially offload, including the FBI headquarters and the main Department of Justice building, after deeming them “not core to government operations.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, MICHAEL R. SISAK and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Republicans hammer mayors of Boston, Chicago, Denver and New York over 'sanctuary city' policies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican members of Congress hammered four Democratic mayors Wednesday about their so-called sanctuary city policies, accusing them of endangering Americans and threatening to prosecute local officials.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and TIM SULLIVAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Keith Richards gets some satisfaction from new award honoring Connecticut residents
item.description: Appearing in a building of books instead of a stadium with amps, Rolling Stones guitarist Keith Richards was honored Wednesday with a new award that recognizes Connecticut residents for their creativity, passion and generosity. Richards, 81, who is British, has kept a home in Connecticut since 1985.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Things to know about avalanches, what causes them and how to stay safe
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — Three skiers are believed dead after an avalanche swept them away and buried them under a pile of snow nearly 10 stories deep in the Alaska backcountry. Each winter about 25 people on average die in avalanches in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: As many top Democrats stay silent on Cuomo mayoral run, an accuser feels betrayed
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Lindsey Boylan helped drive Andrew Cuomo from office in 2021 when the former aide came forward as the first woman to accuse the governor of sexual harassment.
item.dc:creator: BY JAKE OFFENHARTZ and ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: DOJ launches investigation into UC system over antisemitism allegations
item.description: The U.S. Justice Department announced on Wednesday that it has launched a civil investigation into the University of California system under Title VII of the Civil Rights Act. Under Title VII, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump grants one-month exemption for US automakers from new tariffs on imports from Mexico, Canada
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is granting a one-month exemption on his stiff new tariffs on imports from Mexico and Canada for U.S. automakers, as worries persist that the newly launched trade war could crush domestic manufacturing.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Baja Midnight, Quesocrisp Taco, and more: What’s coming to Taco Bell in 2025
item.description: Taco Bell announced a slate of new menu items and plans for the fast food chain at its second annual Live Mas event, which took place in New York City on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Leland Vittert’s War Notes: All My Friends Are Wrong
item.description: NewsNation Chief Washington Anchor and On Balance host Leland Vittert gives you an early look at tonight’s 7 p.m. ET show.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Babysitter accused of drowning child during bathtub baptism
item.description: A Muncie woman was babysitting a close friend's children at her home in Texas when she allegedly decided to baptize the children in the tub.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, WFLA, NBC

item.title: ICE releases criminal histories of illegal immigrant mother and son arrested in California amid backlash
item.description: ICE is defending itself after it arrested a mother and son pair of illegal immigrants with criminal histories in the United States, the agency said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Griffith Observatory to stream total lunar eclipse live online
item.description: Griffith Observatory will host a live online broadcast of the total lunar eclipse on March 13-14, allowing viewers to watch the celestial event remotely. The broadcast will begin at 8:50 p.m. on the Observatory’s YouTube channel and run through 3:05 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Three Michigan brothers missing since 2010 are declared dead by a judge at mom's request
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — A judge declared three Michigan brothers dead Wednesday, more than 14 years after they disappeared at Thanksgiving in a small-town tragedy that remains unsolved despite an explicit belief by investigators that their father is responsible.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The US is killing someone by firing squad for the 1st time in 15 years. Here's a look at the history
item.description: It was a punishment for mutiny in colonial times, a way to discourage desertion during the Civil War and a dose of frontier justice in the Old West. In modern times, some consider it a more humane alternative to lethal injection. The firing squad has a long and thorny history in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ohio lawmaker proposes gym membership tax credit
item.description: “They can get a move away from the pharmaceuticals and focus a little bit more on just lifestyle changes,” he said.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Fahmy, NewsNation

item.title: Florida jewelry heist suspect allegedly swallowed more than $750,000 in Tiffany & Co earrings, photo shows
item.description: A suspect allegedly swallowed more than $750,000 worth of Tiffany & Co. earrings following a jewelry heist at a shopping mall in Orlando, Florida, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mötley Crüe postpones Las Vegas residency due to vocalist Vince Neil's health
item.description: Mötley Crüe announced they are postponing their upcoming Las Vegas residency due to vocalist Vince Neil's health.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, Fox 5

item.title: Bill to ban Planned Parenthood from teaching health in Utah schools advances
item.description: Opponents say the language of the bill is broad enough that it could block large hospital system employees from teaching those school health programs.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Aerts, NewsNation

item.title: Mysterious affliction causing endangered Florida fish to spin and die is back
item.description: Following a roughly nine-month lull, there have been 22 reports of the odd spinning behavior involving sawfish in waters around the Florida Keys since December.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: More states want to stop taxing groceries
item.description: The number of states imposing sales taxes on groceries has shrunk over the years, and the number may decrease further in the coming months
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Pope increases physical therapy, marks start of Lent from hospital
item.description: During the morning he participated in an Ash Wednesday celebration, receiving ashes and Holy Communion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: NYC Mayor Eric Adams, an ex-cop, has 'upper hand' over other sanctuary city mayors: Blue Lives Matter founder
item.description: NYC Mayor Eric Adams has a leg up over his fellow sanctuary city leaders ahead of their House testimony, according to the founder of a pro-police nonprofit.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elissa Slotkin assails Trump's early actions, offers Democrats a way to fight back
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — First-term Michigan Sen. Elissa Slotkin accused President Donald Trump of driving up costs while pushing for an “unprecedented giveaway to his billionaire friends” in Tuesday night's Democratic response to his first joint congressional address of his second term.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's address to Congress
item.description: President Trump delivered the first congressional address of his second term on Tuesday evening. It was a chance for the president to underscore the sweeping changes he has wrought in the six weeks since he returned to the White House — and to defend those moves from his many critics.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Emboldened Trump praises his 'swift action' in address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump will stand before a joint session of Congress on Tuesday to give an accounting of his turbulent first weeks in office as a divided nation struggles to keep pace, with some Americans fearing for the country's future while others are cheering him on.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Joann store closures: See the list of nearly 800 locations headed for sale
item.description: After more than 80 years, national fabric and craft retailer Joann is another step closer to being erased from shopping centers across the U.S. after the release of nearly 800 locations going up for sale in the coming months.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lutnick: Trump may announce tariff compromise with Canada, Mexico on Wednesday
item.description: Trump implementing 25 percent tariffs triggered retaliatory measures from both nations, with major corporations also warning that it could lead to potential price increases for consumers.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, ABC 4

item.title: Florida man swallows $769K in stolen jewelry from Tiffany & Co.: police
item.description: A man was accused of stealing hundreds of thousands of dollars worth of jewelry from a Tiffany & Co. store in Orlando and swallowing the evidence.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Judge tells attorneys to stop being so secretive in Bryan Kohberger's quadruple murder case
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — An Idaho judge is warning attorneys to quit filing so many sealed documents in the murder case of a man accused in the stabbing deaths of four University of Idaho students.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: IRS drafting plans to cut as much as half of its 90,000-person workforce, AP sources say
item.description: The IRS is drafting plans to cut its workforce by as much as half through a mix of layoffs, attrition and incentivized buyouts, according to two people familiar with the situation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump administration ramps up attacks as Zelenskyy thanks US
item.description: There is a growing chorus of Republicans calling for new Ukrainian leadership following Friday's Oval Office meeting.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, Fox 5
